# Move all Garmin and Peloton synchronization, ingestion, calendar ownership, workout writeback, authentication, and scheduling into Training Assistant and retire the sibling garmin-connect-sync repository without behavior or data loss. Spec - Drift Anchor

*Generated by PlanForge V2 on 2026-07-14. Session: `20260714-2058-garmin-retirement`. This is a drift anchor, not a standalone product brief.*

## End State

Training Assistant is the sole runtime owner of Garmin and Peloton integrations, database ownership, refresh/writeback adapters, and scheduled workers; the old sibling repository is not referenced by active runtime, configuration, deployment, tests, or documentation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All current Garmin ingestion domains and Peloton import run from Training Assistant-owned code.
- Existing Garmin identifiers, completed history, races, plan rows, and assistant plan entries are preserved.
- Assistant-owned mode skips generic Garmin calendar workouts and all six current plan workouts remain linked successfully.
- On-demand refresh, scheduled sync, plan generation, approved writeback, dashboard, plan, activity, and race endpoints remain healthy.
- Only one Garmin scheduler is active and no active file references garmin-connect-sync.
- Rollback to the old scheduler is documented and exercised before repository retirement.

## Out Of Scope

- Changing public API or frontend behavior.
- Changing training-plan algorithms or race prioritization.
- Rewriting the Garmin API client for style alone.
- Deleting historical data, token stores, or personal activity exports.

## Speedrift Modules

- coredrift (required): All tasks need baseline drift checks.
- specdrift (required): The approved design is the end-state anchor.
- datadrift (required): The migration owns populated Garmin tables and structured contracts.
- archdrift (required): The work changes the integration boundary and process topology.
- depsdrift (required): Garmin and Peloton dependencies move repositories.
- redrift (required): This is a brownfield parity migration with staged cutover.
- yagnidrift (recommended): The final stage removes obsolete adapters and references.
- fixdrift (recommended): Findings must become bounded repair tasks rather than silent patches.
- reviewdrift (required): Data and runtime cutover need independent review.
- uxdrift (excluded): No user-facing UI behavior is intentionally changed; endpoint regression tests cover the existing surface.

## Verification

- pre-task drift: `./.workgraph/drifts check --task <task_id> --write-log --create-followups` (pre_edit)
- full API suite: `cd api && uv run pytest -q` (pre_handoff)
- legacy reference scan: `cd api && uv run pytest -q tests/test_no_legacy_garmin_reference.py` (pre_handoff)
- post-task drift: `./.workgraph/drifts check --task <task_id> --write-log --create-followups` (per_task)
- Garmin config: `cd api && uv run pytest -q tests/test_garmin_config.py` - Typed settings resolve Training Assistant-owned paths.
- Garmin ingestion: `cd api && uv run pytest -q tests/integrations/test_garmin_ingest.py` - IDempotent activity and summary ingestion is preserved.
- Calendar ownership: `cd api && uv run pytest -q tests/integrations/test_garmin_calendar.py` - Assistant mode skips generic Garmin workouts.
- Schema migration: `cd api && uv run pytest -q tests/test_garmin_schema_migration.py tests/test_db.py` - Clean and populated database paths preserve identifiers.
- API regression: `cd api && uv run pytest -q` - Refresh, writeback, plan, dashboard, activities, and races remain healthy.
- Live cutover smoke: `curl -k -fsS https://braydons-macbook-pro.tail277a09.ts.net:3572/api/v1/plan/current` - Tailnet plan and dashboard endpoints return 200 after cutover.
- waiver UI interaction tests: No frontend behavior changes are planned; endpoint and existing application regression tests cover the preserved surface. (approved by user-approved design)

## Risks

- Populated Garmin tables differ from repository models. [high]: Snapshot and verify before and after a non-destructive Alembic migration.
- New and old schedulers write simultaneously. [high]: Use one lock and make scheduler cutover a separate gated operation.
- Writeback creates Garmin duplicates or leaves assistant entries stale. [high]: Test atomic ID/status updates and compare Garmin IDs during canary.
- Authentication fails after token-store move. [medium]: Preserve token store during migration and run a live read-only smoke before writes.
- Legacy references survive in operational documentation. [medium]: Run a no-reference test across active source, scripts, deploy files, tests, and README.
